questions.question: What is CORS, Errors & Debugging APIs and why is it important?
questions.answer: CORS, Errors & Debugging APIs is a fundamental concept that Cross-origin requests, CORS headers, error handling, and API debugging. It's important because it forms the foundation for building robust and efficient applications. Understanding CORS, Errors & Debugging APIs allows developers to make better architectural decisions and write cleaner code.

questions.question: How do you apply CORS, Errors & Debugging APIs in a real project?
questions.answer: In practice, CORS, Errors & Debugging APIs is applied by following established patterns and best practices. Start with the simplest implementation, test thoroughly, and iterate. The key is to understand the underlying principles so you can adapt to different scenarios.

questions.question: What are common mistakes when working with CORS, Errors & Debugging APIs?
questions.answer: Common mistakes include: not understanding the fundamentals before applying advanced patterns, ignoring edge cases, not testing adequately, and over-engineering solutions. The best approach is to start simple and add complexity only when needed.

questions.question: How would you explain CORS, Errors & Debugging APIs to a beginner?
questions.answer: CORS, Errors & Debugging APIs is essentially about cross-origin requests, cors headers, error handling, and api debugging. Think of it as a building block — once you understand this concept, you can combine it with other concepts to build complex applications.
